title: Keshabeshwar temple
tagline: Keshabeshwar temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Keshabeshwar Temple is a Chala style Hindu temple at Ramnathpur village.
overview.description: Keshabeshwar Temple is a Chala style Hindu temple at Ramnathpur village. It is located on the western side of the road and is dedicated to the Hindu deity Shiva. Charak Puja and fair are held every year in the temple premises. In the middle of the 18th century, the local zamindar Keshab Roychowdhury started the construction of the temple for the purpose of establishing and worshiping the Shiva-lingam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Keshabeshwar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Keshabeshwar temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Keshabeshwar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (57 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (48 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Keshabeshwar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Keshabeshwar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Keshabeshwar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Keshabeshwar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Keshabeshwar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mandirbazar (community development block), Mandirbazar, Krishnapur, Mandirbazar, Chandpur, Mandirbazar, Lakshmikantapur.
